HDFC BANK | APPOINTS RAJIV KUMAR AS PART-TIME CHAIRMAN

• HDFC Bank has appointed Rajiv Kumar as Part-Time Chairman, subject to RBI approval.

• The appointment will take effect from the date of approval by the Reserve Bank of India.

• Rajiv Kumar is a former Finance Secretary and former Chief Election Commissioner of India.

• He has been credited with leading key banking reforms, PSU bank recapitalisation, NPA resolution, and governance initiatives during his tenure as Finance Secretary.

• He will not be liable to retire by rotation and is not related to any Director or Key Managerial Personnel of the bank.

ACCORD TRANSFORMER & SWITCHGEAR | WINS ₹19.97 CRORE ORDER

• Accord Transformer & Switchgear has secured a ₹19.97 crore domestic order from a leading private sector EPC company.

• The order includes the supply of 3.6 MVA, 33kV/690V and 3.6 MVA, 33kV/950V Wind Turbine Transformers.

• The transformers will be supplied for a wind power project in Gadag, Karnataka.

• The order is scheduled to be executed within 5 months.

KWALITY WALL'S | SIGNS 3-YEAR IP AGREEMENT WITH MAGNUM IP

• The Board has approved a 3-year Intellectual Property Agreement with Magnum IP Holdings B.V., valid until March 31, 2029.

• Kwality Wall's will pay 0% royalty on licensed products until March 31, 2027, supporting post-demerger investments and business stabilisation.

• A 1% royalty on net sales (plus applicable taxes) will apply for FY28 and FY29.

• The agreement grants the company the right to use Magnum IP's intellectual property for the sale of licensed products in India.

ASTER DM HEALTHCARE | INVESTS ₹25 CRORE IN ASSOCIATE

• Aster DM Healthcare has invested ₹25 crore in associate Alfaone Medicals Pvt. Ltd. through a rights issue.

• The company acquired 3,57,143 OCRPS at ₹700 per OCRPS.

• The investment has been made for the general corporate purposes of the associate.

• Aster continues to hold a 48.91% equity stake in Alfaone Medicals, while its OCRPS holding has increased to 100% (37,54,243 OCRPS).

RAILTEL | WINS ₹27.06 CRORE ORDER FROM GOA LABOUR WELFARE BOARD

• RailTel Corporation of India has secured a ₹27.06 crore (including taxes) domestic order from the Goa Labour Welfare Board.

• The order is for the development of an exclusive end-to-end online portal.

• The project is scheduled to be completed by August 23, 2026.

• RailTel confirmed that the order is domestic in nature and that there is no promoter interest in the awarding entity.

CREST VENTURES | SUBSIDIARY SIGNS DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT FOR ₹2,200 CRORE DADAR PROJECT

• Crest Ventures, through its wholly owned subsidiary Sutlej Housing Pvt. Ltd., has signed a Development Agreement for a cluster redevelopment project in Dadar East, Mumbai.

VEDANTA GROUP STOCKS
Circuit Revised

ABS Marine Services Secures ~ ₹ 126.12 Cr Long-Term Charter Contract for Offshore Support Vessel MV ARTEMIS

ABS Marine Services Limited, one of the leading maritime companies offering comprehensive services in Vessel Ownership, Ship Management, Marine and Port Services has signed a Charter Party Agreement with Hardy Exploration & Production (India) Inc., acting on behalf of itself and its joint venture partners, Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC) and Invenire Petrodyne Limited, for the deployment of its Anchor Handling Tug Supply (AHTS) Vessel, MV ARTEMIS.

Under the agreement, MV ARTEMIS will be deployed to support petroleum exploration and production activities on the east coast of India. The contract has a firm charter tenure of 1,825 days (5 years) from the date of mobilisation, with an option for extension by up to a further 1,825 days at the charterer's discretion. The contract is valued at approximately ₹126.12 Cr (inclusive of GST) over the firm charter period.

Strategic Impact
The contract reinforces the company’s position as a trusted offshore service provider and enhances revenue visibility through a long-term charter arrangement. The deployment of MV ARTEMIS under a multi-year contract is expected to support stable vessel utilization and strengthen the Company's offshore operations portfolio.

The award also reflects the Company's proven operational capabilities and strong relationships within the offshore energy sector. With increasing focus on domestic exploration and offshore energy development, the company remains well positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities and further expand its presence in the offshore support services segment.

reports.text:
*Delhi EV Policy 2026-2030 – At a glance*
Cabinet approved it today, rollout likely from July 1, 2026. Here are the key points:

*1. Registration bans / deadlines*
- *3-wheelers*: Only electric 3-wheelers for new registrations from *1 Jan 2027*. CNG/petrol auto registrations stop.
- *2-wheelers*: Only electric 2-wheelers for new registrations from *1 Apr 2028*. Existing petrol bikes are exempt.
- *Fleets/Aggregators*: No new ICE 2W + LCVs up to 3.5 ton from *1 Jan 2026*. BS-VI 2Ws allowed till 31 Dec 2026.
- *Govt fleet*: All new hired/leased vehicles, DTC buses, MCD/NDMC trucks to be electric only.

*2. Tax & registration benefits till 31 Mar 2030*
- *100% road tax + registration waiver* for EVs up to ₹30 lakh ex-showroom. EVs above ₹30 lakh get no waiver.
- *50% waiver* for strong hybrid cars up to ₹30 lakh - first time in Delhi. 

*3. Purchase incentives via DBT*
- *2-wheelers*: ₹10,000/kWh up to ₹30,000 in Year 1, tapering to ₹6,600/kWh + ₹3,300/kWh in next 2 years. Cap: ₹2.25 lakh ex-factory price.
- *E-autos*: ₹50,000 Yr1 → ₹40,000 Yr2 → ₹30,000 Yr3.
- *E-goods carriers N1*: ₹1 lakh Yr1 → ₹75,000 Yr2 → ₹50,000 Yr3.
- *Cars*: No direct subsidy. *Scrappage incentive ₹1 lakh* for scrapping BS-IV & below cars when buying EV ≤₹30 lakh. Limited to first 1 lakh applicants.
- *Conversion kit*: ₹50,000 grant to convert petrol/diesel car to EV with certified kit. 

*4. Infrastructure push*
- *7,000 new charging points + 100 battery swapping stations* in 2026. Target: 16,000+ total charging stations by year-end.
- *Dealers mandate*: All 400+ vehicle dealerships must install ≥1 public charger.
- *Delhi Transco* nodal agency for public charging. Single-window clearance proposed. 

*5. Battery recycling & environment*
- Strong focus on *Extended Producer Responsibility, collection centres, traceability* for battery disposal. DPCC to enforce strict rules. 51fe0fd3

*6. Budget*
- Total outlay *₹3,954.25 crore*: ₹1,236 cr purchase incentives, ₹1,718 cr scrapping, ₹1,000 cr charging infra. 

*Bottom line*: Delhi is shifting from "incentives only" to "deadline + incentives". 2W/3W buyers get max benefit if you switch early. Car buyers get tax waiver + scrappage, but no direct subsidy.

reports.text:
Delhivery: Management on Logistics & Quick Commerce

Key Management Views
- Logistics sector heading for consolidation
- Market can support only a few large players
- Rising costs squeezing smaller operators
- Focus remains on profitable growth

Logistics Outlook
- Strong positions in third-party logistics
- Limited incremental market volume
- Fuel and labour costs rising
- Further industry consolidation expected

Quick Commerce View
- Blinkit profitability difficult to replicate
- Industry still dependent on subsidies
- Shared infrastructure could improve economics
- Delhivery avoiding standalone last-mile delivery

Growth Strategy
- Focus on upstream supply chain
- 2–4 hour delivery for D2C brands
- Integrated logistics network
- Disciplined capital allocation

Why It Matters
- Highlights industry consolidation trend
- Reinforces focus on profitability over growth
- Suggests caution on quick commerce economics
- Positive for established logistics leaders

Key Takeaway
- Delhivery's management expects further consolidation in India's logistics industry, believing the market can sustainably support only a handful of large players. The company remains cautious on quick commerce, arguing that Blinkit's profitability may be difficult for peers to replicate, and will continue focusing on its core logistics and supply chain business rather than entering high-burn last-mile delivery.

Key Developments
- GLP-1 demand exceeds manufacturing capacity
- CDMOs expanding production facilities
- Supply constraints may last 2–3 years
- Semaglutide patent expiry driving demand

Key Bottlenecks
- Peptide API manufacturing
- Sterile fill-finish capacity
- Auto-injector devices
- Glass cartridges & delivery systems
- Regulatory approvals (DMFs)

Industry Trends
- Manufacturing slots booked 12–24 months ahead
- Strong export enquiries increasing
- Africa, West Asia & Latin America demand rising
- Long-term demand remains robust

Why Capacity Takes Time
- Facility construction: ~2 years
- Validation & qualification: 3–6 months
- Regulatory approvals: 18–24 months
- Commercial supply delayed despite expansion

Why It Matters
- Strong revenue visibility for CDMOs
- High capacity utilization expected
- Long-term outsourcing opportunity
- Structural growth in GLP-1 manufacturing

Key Takeaway
- The GLP-1 manufacturing ecosystem is facing a structural supply shortage, with bottlenecks across peptide APIs, sterile fill-finish, injector devices, and regulatory approvals. Although Indian CDMOs are aggressively expanding capacity, supply constraints are expected to persist for the next 2–3 years, providing strong demand visibility and long-term growth opportunities for manufacturers with compliant infrastructure.

Persistent Systems🔻8% mainly due to the market’s reaction to its Nagarro acquisition

Company has announced an all cash offer to acquire Nagarro SE at €81/share (acquired a significant premium)

Europe is the key strategic angle here. Persistent has been looking for a complementary acquisition in Europe, and Nagarro helps solve that gap. The combined company will have more than $600 Mn REV from Europe, along with more than $1.7 Bn from North America and over $400 Mn from the rest of the world.

Deal is also looks EPS accretive. On an illustrative pro forma TTM basis, the combined entity would have REV of $2,795 Mn, EBITDA of $463 Mn, EBIT of $337 Mn & PAT of $215 Mn.

The transaction assumes total debt of nearly $1.5 Bn, including acquisition funding & refinancing Nagarro’s existing debt.

Another concern is margins. Nagarro’s reported CY25 EBITDA margin was 12%. After adjustments, the margin improves to around 14-15%.

= ₹381/share

Current REC Price: ₹365

Implied Discount
₹381 − ₹365 = ₹16/share
= ~4.4% merger arbitrage discount

WHAT IT MEANS

- If you wish to participate in merger entity, buy REC shares
- 4.4% gain if you buy REC shares over PFC at current market price

Western battery makers try to keep bad cells below 1 per million. CATL, which makes roughly 4 in every 10 EV batteries sold worldwide, aims for 1 bad cell per billion, a thousand times stricter. 

They run AI across 6,800 quality checkpoints on their production lines, A single EV pack holds thousands of cells and one defective cell can start a fire. 

The gap between measuring quality per million versus per billion is not a numbers game, it is an entirely different manufacturing culture, and right now nobody in the West is close to matching it.

CATL chief technology officer for 8 years was Bob Galyen, who spent 21 years at General Motors. BMW 2012 contract forced CATL to meet German automotive quality standards when it was still a small company. 

This follows the same path Japan car industry took. Toyota learned quality systems from an American consultant, then used that discipline to outcompete Detroit. 

CATL did something similar, absorbed Western expertise and Western client standards, then scaled manufacturing precision to a level those same Western companies have not been able to match on their own

LG defective battery cells in the Chevy Bolt caused fires and forced a recall of every Bolt ever made, costing around $2 billion. 

Root cause was two manufacturing defects appearing in the same cell, exactly the kind of failure CATL billion level quality checks are designed to catch, CATL now holds 40% of the global EV battery market. 

When one bad batch can cost you $2 billion and your entire brand reputation, quality is not a department you fund. It is the thing that decides whether you survive in this industry at all.

**Neogen Chemicals**

Neogen Chemicals targets Rs.2,400–2,900 crores revenue from battery chemicals by FY29

Neogen strategically entered battery chemicals around 2017, driven by rising lithium prices and the global shift towards EV batteries

Electrolyte was chosen as a core focus because it is central to the battery, recipe-driven, has shelf-life constraints, and is difficult to import at scale

The battery chemicals business aims for a 20% pre-tax Return on Capital (ROC), utilizing a formula-based pricing model that tracks lithium price movements

The revenue plan is heavily dependent on the battery cell manufacturing ecosystem scaling up significantly in India, with at least 60 GWh expected in 1.5-2 years and 150 GWh by FY29/FY30

Neogen's formula-based pricing for electrolyte is projected to be more competitive than China's spot prices over a five-year horizon, offering customers transparency and sourcing flexibility

Electrolyte import at gigawatt scale is deemed impractical due to logistics, temperature stability, and shelf-life constraints, emphasizing the need for local manufacturing

The primary risk identified is a potential delay in India's overall battery manufacturing ramp-up, rather than technology failure, though management expresses optimism

Source:- Phillip Capital

Emerald Finance Limited Onboards Ebixcash Mobility Software India Limited, Delhi for Early Wage Access

Emerald Finance Limited has entered into a new employer partnership with Ebixcash Mobility Software India Ltd., Delhi, extending its Early-Wage-Access (EWA) program to their employees.

Through a salary-linked advance model, employees can access a portion of their earned wages at any point during the month - disbursed in coordination with the employer, helping address short-term liquidity needs.

By eliminating dependency on the fixed pay cycle, the program gives employees greater control over their finances. Repayments are managed through automatic payroll deductions at month end - keeping the process disciplined, transparent and stress-free.

This collaboration reflects Emerald Finance's continued commitment to expanding its EWA network across India, making earned wage access a standard employee benefit.

MOTILAL ON SRF

Maintains BUY with TP ₹3,350

Fluorochemicals hit a record in FY26 with 60% revenue growth, and a ₹23b capex in next-gen refrigerants (HFO) sets up a multi-year growth engine. 

Specialty Chemicals dipped due to agrochem weakness but recovery expected from FY27. EBITDA margins recovered to 22.8%, PAT jumped 49% YoY to ₹20.4b. 

We expect 22% PAT CAGR through FY28 with EPS hitting ₹101. 

Stock at 27x FY28 earnings looks attractive for a quality specialty chemicals compounder.

**Jefferies on HDFC AMC**

**Rating**
- Buy
- Target Price: ₹3,090/share

**Management Takeaways**
- May slowdown not a concern
- Followed two months of strong inflows
- Core business trends remain stable
- Long-term outlook unchanged

**Margin Outlook**
- TER changes passed through ecosystem
- No material margin impact
- Profitability remains resilient
- Operating leverage intact

**Growth Initiatives**
- Expanding investment team
- Strengthening portfolio performance
- Scaling GIFT City platform
- Growing AIF & PMS businesses
- Expanding frontline sales force

**Financial Outlook**
- AUM growth: ~21% (2 years)
- Operating profit growth: ~16% (2 years)
- Healthy earnings visibility
- Multiple growth drivers

**Valuation**
- Trading at 42x FY27E OPAT
- ~13% discount to peers
- Attractive relative valuation
- Potential for re-rating

**Key Takeaway**
- Jefferies reiterates its **Buy** rating with a **₹3,090 target**, citing stable core trends, no margin impact from TER changes, expansion into GIFT/AIF/PMS businesses, and expected **21% AUM** and **16% operating profit growth** over the next two years.

**InCred on Non-Ferrous**

**Sector View**
- Negative on aluminium
- Bull case appears overstated
- Valuations look stretched
- 30-40% downside risk flagged

**Key Thesis**
- Focus on primary supply misplaced
- Scrap & secondary aluminium ignored
- Supply tightness easing
- Commodity outlook weakening

**China Outlook**
- Primary deficit narrowing
- Secondary consumption: 13.35 Mt (2025)
- Scrap replacing primary output
- Better overall supply balance

**Global Factors**
- Middle East risk premium fading
- Supply disruptions temporary
- Aluminium prices vulnerable
- Potential correction: ~$800/tonne

**Stocks in Focus**
- NALCO
- Vedanta Aluminium
- Hindalco Industries

**Investment View**
- Rich valuations
- Earnings risk if prices fall
- Commodity cycle turning
- Limited upside at current levels

**Key Takeaway**
- InCred remains **negative on the aluminium sector**, arguing that rising secondary supply in China and fading geopolitical risk could drive an **~$800/tonne correction** in aluminium prices, leaving stocks like **NALCO, Vedanta Aluminium, and Hindalco** exposed to **30-40% downside**.

**Morgan Stanley on Adani Power**

**Rating**
- Overweight
- Target Price: ₹275/share (Raised)

**Investment Thesis**
- Lower-risk growth profile
- High return-accretive projects
- Fastest growth among peers
- Strong execution track record

**Business Transformation**
- Shift toward PPA-led portfolio
- Stable annuity-like cash flows
- Reduced merchant exposure
- Higher earnings visibility

**Capacity Expansion**
- 24 GW under construction
- Majority backed by PPAs
- EBITDA: ~₹3.5/kWh
- Fuel cost pass-through available

**Competitive Advantage**
- Strong commissioning history
- Healthy leverage position
- Early equipment procurement
- Execution edge over peers

**Cost Advantage**
- Equipment pre-ordered from BHEL & L&T
- Capex 10-30% lower than peers
- Greater bidding flexibility
- Supports future PPA wins

**Growth Pipeline**
- Upcoming 14 GW PPAs
- Uttar Pradesh
- Rajasthan
- Uttarakhand
- West Bengal
- Gujarat

**Key Risks**
- Weak power demand
- Lower merchant realization (FY27-FY28)
- Project commissioning delays
- Capex inflation
- Rising DISCOM receivables

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley maintains an **Overweight** rating and raises its target price to **₹275**, highlighting Adani Power's transition toward a lower-risk, PPA-backed annuity model, supported by superior execution, lower project costs, and strong long-term earnings visibility.

reports.text:
MACQUARIE ON INDIA CONSUMER

2019 monsoon season is instructive where while onset of monsoon was poor

In 2019, monsoon in Jun at 67%, while in July 105%, Aug 115%, Sep 152% of LPA

Resulting in no limited impact to agri production/ rural incomes

See rural focused plays like Britannia, Dabur at risk from potential hit to monsoons

Preferred plays in the consumer space include Titan, Lenskart, Marico and HUL

JEFFERIES ON REAL ESTATE

Industry wide double-digit sales growth in Apr-May

Easing geopolitical tensions has led to late quarter push for approvals by listed comp

Major launches by Oberoi, Prestige, GPL, Sobha, DLF are skewing late June/early July

Depending on how late qtr. launches get booked, we expect 20% + sales growth

See sub 10% for Lodha, Large decline for Prestige Estates and DLF YOY

JEFFERIES ON SYNGENE AND CONCORD

Recently interacted with Syngene and Concord Biotech management

Syngene identified gaps impacting RFP conversion & initiated corrective actions

FY27 will be a transition year for Syngene due to ongoing destocking impacts

Concord's FY26 weakness was driven by US destocking

Oncology, anti-infectives expected to be key near-term growth drivers for Concord
